blockers: codex has required edits: Correct the shared lexer’s regex-versus-division state so every supported expression-start context is handled, including at least `await`, `yield`, and statement/block boundaries, without stale keyword state after strings, templates, regexes, or other value tokens. Add fixtures proving regex lookalikes produce no edge and genuine division followed by a load remains visible across these contexts.; Enforce the exact AM-41 profile with `ProfileError` and diagnostic `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`: validate complete `\xHH`, `\uHHHH`, and bounded/nonempty `\u{…}` escapes; detect line-leading `-->` after permitted leading whitespace; and structurally reject every truncated accepted import/export/call form, including unclosed clauses and malformed dynamic-import option/trailing-comma structures. Extend b1–b6 tests with these counterexamples and assert the stable diagnostic.; Make original-component inspection platform-safe before any normalization. Either reject backslashes in relative module specifiers deterministically before filesystem resolution or tokenize and inspect both platform separator spellings in original order. Add a symlink-component-plus-later-`..` fixture using backslash separators on Windows, alongside the existing forward-slash fixture.; Remove the `from` default-binding exclusion in `parseImportClause` while retaining correct governing-`from` detection. Add a code-weaver fixture for `import from from "./dep.mjs"` with an actual use and assert the appropriate file-level dependency endpoint.; After the shared scanner/resolver corrections, re-run the real git/code closure derivation and retain exact committed-inventory equality and deterministic-output tests.

codex has hard stops: AM-41 supported-profile correctness is not met by the lexer’s regex/division logic. `REGEX_KEYWORDS` omits valid expression-start contexts such as `await` and `yield`, and the lexer lacks sufficient block-versus-expression context after `}`. A valid source such as `async function f(){ await /import(".\x2fevil.mjs")/; }` can therefore be interpreted as containing a literal dynamic import instead of a regex lookalike, corrupting D33 closure derivation.; The exact fail-closed source profile is incompletely enforced. Malformed supported-prefix escapes such as `\xG0`, `\uZZZZ`, and `\u{}` can pass without `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`, while an out-of-range `\u{...}` can escape as a native `RangeError`. Whitespace-prefixed line-leading `-->` is not detected, and truncated accepted declarations such as `import { x }` or `export *` return no edge rather than failing closed. Unbalanced dynamic-import option structures can likewise be accepted when a closing parenthesis is present.; AM-41(c) original-component containment is bypassable on Windows. `originalChainSymlinkFree` splits only on `/`, then passes an embedded backslash-containing segment to `path.join`, which can normalize `link\..` before `lstat`. A specifier equivalent to `./link\..\real\target.mjs` can erase a symlink component before inspection, contrary to the requirement to inspect every original component before normalization.; The code-weaver import parser drops the valid contextual default binding `from`: `parseImportClause` explicitly excludes `from` after correctly classifying `import from from "./dep.mjs"`. Consequently, a used import in this frozen supported form emits no required file-level `depends-on` edge.

acceptance.note: Fail-closed: see gate.blockers and seat decisions.
